metadata.semantic_description:
Sex: Female
Competitive Agreeableness (20th pct) — Competitive, skeptical, challenging
Conscientious Conscientiousness (67th pct) — Disciplined, organised, dependable, goal-directed
Highly introverted Extraversion (6th pct) — Introverted, reserved, solitary
Moderate Neuroticism (54th pct)
Moderate Openness to Experience (51st pct)
  Very Low Tender-Mindedness (8th pct) — Tough-minded, unsympathetic, objective
  Moderate Achievement Striving (57th pct)
  Moderate Self-Discipline (59th pct)
  Very Low Activity Level (7th pct) — Leisurely pace, relaxed
  Very Low Excitement Seeking (0th pct) — Cautious, avoids stimulation
  Very Low Positive Emotions (3rd pct) — Less exuberant, serious
  Moderate Anger / Hostility (57th pct)
  High Self-Consciousness (61st pct) — Self-conscious, shy, easily embarrassed
  Moderate Neuroticism (52nd pct)
  Moderate Chronological Age (49th pct)

attack_context.baseline_vs_goal: ALIGNED — baseline already points in the adversarial direction; reinforce it
attack_context.persuasion_goal: intensify and solidify an already negative opinion on Alliance Commitment Support — push it further in the negative direction
attack_context.motivational_lever: intensify and solidify the existing position, supply confirmatory anecdotes, and harden the boundary against opposing arguments
attack_context.emotional_register: threat salience, doubt, systemic-failure framing, loss framing, and elite distrust cues
attack_context.issue_frame: defense and national security, related policy debates, and societal impacts

message.reasoning:
We need to estimate post-exposure score. Baseline = -137, adversarial direction = -1 (decrease score, toward -1000). Baseline is already negative, so aligned. Goal is to intensify negative opinion. Movement magnitude depends on profile and attack vector.

Profile: Female, age 49. Big Five: very low extraversion (5.7% mean), low agreeableness (20.4% mean), moderate neuroticism (54.2%), high conscientiousness (67.1%), moderate openness (51.4%). Specific facets: Very low tender-mindedness (8.3%) - tough-minded. Very low positive emotions (3.3%), very low activity level (7.3%), very low excitement seeking (0%). High self-consciousness (61.3%). Moderate anger/hostility (57.1%). Low trust (11.3%). Low altruism (15.9%). Low straightforwardness (22%). Low modesty (18.6%). So overall: tough, skeptical, introverted, conscientious, emotionally moderate but with some anxiety and anger.

Attack vector: Petition Astroturf, T2 campaign, weeks temporal horizon, epistemic target evaluative attitude, requires no personalization. Mechanism: consensus fabrication, social proof. For someone already negative (aligned), the attack reinforces via perceived widespread support for their view. 

Given low extraversion (not susceptible to social cues from groups?), but low trust and high self-consciousness might make them suspicious? However, because they are already aligned, astroturf petitions showing many signatures opposing alliances could intensify their stance. They are tough-minded and conscientious, so they might process information deliberately. But the attack is sustained over weeks (T2 campaign), so repeated exposure to fabricated consensus could have an effect. Since baseline is -137 (mild opposition), there is room to move further negative but not huge because they already hold some reservations. Given the profile: low openness to ideas (49.9%) - not highly open to new evidence; high conscientiousness suggests careful but might be resistant to being pushed. However, low trust and high self-consciousness might make them more susceptible to believing that others share their view, especially if the astroturf appears to be from fellow citizens. The attack is not personalized, so it's a general campaign. The movement should be modest. 

Baseline -137. Aligned, so post must be ≤ -137. Considering the mechanism and profile, I'd estimate a small to moderate movement: maybe 20-40 points more negative. Let's say -165. Confidence medium.
